questions.text: Which of the following is not present in RNA?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Uracil is indeed present in RNA. It is one of the four nitrogenous bases in RNA, pairing with adenine in RNA during the process of transcription.

RNA (ribonucleic acid) is a nucleic acid that consists of a chain of nucleotides. Each nucleotide in RNA consists of three components: a phosphate group, a ribose sugar molecule, and a nitrogenous base. The nitrogenous bases in RNA are adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C), and uracil (U).
Ribose is present in RNA. It is the sugar component of the RNA nucleotides. RNA uses ribose instead of deoxyribose, which is found in DNA.
Phosphate is also present in RNA. It is a crucial component of RNA nucleotides, providing the negatively charged backbone of the RNA molecule.

questions.explanation: RNA (ribonucleic acid) is a nucleic acid that consists of a chain of nucleotides. Each nucleotide in RNA consists of three components: a phosphate group, a ribose sugar molecule, and a nitrogenous base. The nitrogenous bases in RNA are adenine (A), guanine (G), cytosine (C), and uracil (U).

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chrome oxidase, also known as cytochrome e oxidase, is an enzyme found in the mitochondrial membrane, It is the last enzyme in the respiratory electron transport chain and plays a pivotal role incellular respiration. The enzyme contains iron (Fe) as a part of its heme groups, which ar= essentialfor its function The iron ions facilitate the transfer of electrons from gyrochrome c to oxygen, thefinal electron acceptor, resulting in the formation of water. This process is crucial for the production of ATP, the energy currency of the cell.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Steroids are a class of organic compounds that have a characteristic four-ring structure. The structure shown in the image is a basic steroid structure, consisting of three six-membered rings and one five-membered ring.

questions.explanation: Steroids are a class of organic compounds that have a characteristic four-ring structure. The structure shown in the image is a basic steroid structure, consisting of three six-membered rings and one five-membered ring.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fructose is a monosaccharide, a simple sugar with the molecular formula C_6H_12O_6. It is a ketohexose, meaning it has a ketone functional group and six carbon atoms.

questions.explanation: Fructose is a monosaccharide, a simple sugar with the molecular formula C_6H_12O_6. It is a ketohexose, meaning it has a ketone functional group and six carbon atoms.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a disaccharide?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Disaccharides are carbohydrates composed of two monosaccharide units joined together by a glycosidic bond.Sucrose, lactose, and raffinose are all disaccharides.Dextrin is a polysaccharide, meaning it is made up of many monosaccharide units linked together.

questions.explanation: Disaccharides are carbohydrates composed of two monosaccharide units joined together by a glycosidic bond.Sucrose, lactose, and raffinose are all disaccharides.Dextrin is a polysaccharide, meaning it is made up of many monosaccharide units linked together.

questions.text: Which of the following has less amount of protein
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Fruits are generally low in protein compared to other food sources like potatoes, milk, and eggs. Fruits are primarily composed of carbohydrates and fiber.

questions.explanation: Fruits are generally low in protein compared to other food sources like potatoes, milk, and eggs. Fruits are primarily composed of carbohydrates and fiber.

questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Chrome oxidase is indeed an enzyme that utilizes iron as a cofactor. It's involved in the electron transport chain, specifically in the reduction of oxygen to water. The iron atom in the heme group of chrome oxidase plays a crucial role in this process.Why it's correct: Chrome oxidase relies on the iron atom in its heme group to facilitate electron transfer during the reduction of oxygen.
